artPrompt: A tall view of a back-room gaming table where a single six-sided die tumbles through a shaft of amber lamplight, shadowed goblin players ringed around it bleeding into ink-black, the dice glow the only certainty. Cigar smoke and smoky-brown washes swallow the edges, the rigged outcome implied in the loaded weight of the throw. An ongoing crooked enchantment, rendered as loose ink-and-watercolor — confident brush linework, bleeding translucent washes, a limited palette, generous negative space.
name: The Numbers Game
text:
When The Numbers Game enters the battlefield, each player rolls a six-sided die. Each opponent who rolled lower than you owes you a Favor (you create that many Favor tokens). The numbers always come up for the house.
Whenever a player rolls a 1 on a die, you create a Favor token and they get nothing. Snake eyes belong to the syndicate.
flavorText: It's called the numbers game because the numbers are the game, and the game is rigged, and the rig is the point.
